Round 40.decision_summary: Phase 2 walking skeleton lands — `crates/` Cargo workspace + `wz-codecs` crate that hosts sce-codegen Rust output for the §6 payload trio (msg_put + msg_del + 5 deps). First piece of running code in the project — cargo build passes, smoke test exercises encode/decode round-trip on default instances. 38 rounds of spec authoring now have a Rust binary that compiles.
changes_bullets: crates/Cargo.toml new — wz Rust workspace root. resolver=2; members=[wz-codecs]. Workspace package shares license + edition + rust-version. [workspace.lints.rust] warnings = "deny" for production gate parity with SCE workspace.

crates/wz-codecs/ new (4 files): Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/lib.rs + tests/smoke.rs. Cargo.toml declares deps on sce-forge-runtime (path) and sce-build (build-dep, path) into vendor/sce. lib.rs declares 8 `pub mod <stem> { include!() }` blocks at crate root for codegen-output sibling resolution.

crates/wz-codecs/build.rs invokes sce-build::compile_forge_with_imports() in-process at compile time, mirroring vendor/sce/sce-forge-runtime/rust/build.rs pattern. 8 SCXMLs compiled in dep-order: timestamp/encoding/ext_unit/ext_zint/ext_zbuf/ext_entry/msg_put/msg_del. cargo:rerun-if-changed wired on sources/codecs/.

SCE codegen template bug workaround in build.rs post-process: SCE emits `#![doc = "SCE-MAP: stem:line"]` as a file-leading inner attribute. When wz includes the file inside `pub mod X { include!(...); }`, rust rejects (E0658 / inner-attribute-not-permitted at item position). SCE's own `cargo test -p sce-forge-runtime --features alloc` hits the same wall — verified R40. Workaround strips the redundant inner-attr line; the SCE-MAP info itself is also emitted on the very next line as a regular `// SCE-MAP: stem:line` comment, so zero info loss. Proper fix is an SCE upstream PR (R41 carry — see below).

tests/smoke.rs (66 lines, 2 tests): msg_put + msg_del Default→encode→decode→assert-fields-match round-trip. Establishes the encode/decode self-consistency baseline that Layer 3 (wire-interop against zenoh-pico bytes) will graduate from in R41/R42.

.gitignore extended: `crates/target/` ignored (workspace build output). `crates/Cargo.lock` COMMITTED per Cargo's binary-producing-workspace recommendation for reproducible Layer 3 builds across CI/dev/prod.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs: pass (Compiling wz-codecs v0.1.0, Finished 'dev' profile, 0 errors, 0 warnings).

cargo test -p wz-codecs: 2 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored. Both msg_put + msg_del default encode→decode round-trips assert all fields match (header byte, payload_len, payload bytes, present-if Optional gates all None).

sce-codegen output (in OUT_DIR): 8 .rs files (timestamp.rs, encoding.rs, ext_unit.rs, ext_zint.rs, ext_zbuf.rs, ext_entry.rs, msg_put.rs, msg_del.rs). All `use super::X::Y;` cross-module references resolve through wz_codecs::lib's `pub mod X { include!() }` declarations.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 39→40, T3 warn 856 unchanged.
First running Rust code in the project. Prior 38 rounds were spec authoring (12 atomic docs, 36 SCXML, vendored SCE, mnemosyne adoption); R40 is the FIRST round whose deliverable executes.
carry_forward_bullets: SCE upstream PR carry — `tools/codegen/templates/forge/rust/<kind>.rs.jinja2` change template to emit SCE-MAP info as `/// SCE-MAP: stem:line` (outer doc, attaches to first struct) or just `// SCE-MAP: stem:line` (regular comment) instead of `#![doc = ...]` (inner attribute). Inner attribute on a file consumed via `include!(...)` inside `pub mod X` is rejected by rust E0658 — broken in SCE's own `--features alloc --tests` build too, just not caught because default build doesn't trigger include!(). Single-line template change; would let wz-codecs/build.rs drop the post-process strip.

R41 = `crates/zenoh-pico-sys` — vendor zenoh-pico as git submodule (sibling to vendor/sce/), build via cc/cmake, generate FFI bindings via bindgen, expose `_z_put_encode` + `_z_keep_alive_encode` + `_z_init_encode` + sibling `_*_decode` Rust signatures. This is the foundation for Layer 3 wire-interop tests.

R42 = `crates/wz-integration-tests` — Layer 3 test crate. For each codec in wz-codecs, construct a logical message instance, encode via wz-codecs Rust path AND via zenoh-pico-sys FFI path, byte-compare. First REAL validation that the codec SCXMLs match zenoh-pico wire-format. This is the round that PROVES the 36 SCXML rounds correctly modeled the wire protocol; if it fails, the diagnosis loop is short because each codec is independently testable.
R43+ = expand wz-codecs to host all 21 codec SCXMLs (currently only 8 of 21 wired). Layer 3 coverage grows in lockstep.
R45+ (post-Phase 2) = runtime crates wz-runtime-tokio + wz-runtime-lwip per the design docs. Session FSM (R29 statechart) wiring becomes possible once runtime trait surface lands.

Production gate carry: GitHub Actions CI (validate_workspace + verify-codegen.sh + cargo test + Layer 3 interop) lands in Phase 4 (R46+ after walking skeleton + a few codec/FSM expansion rounds), per the R39 architectural roadmap.

Round 41.decision_summary: Phase 2 walking-skeleton FFI bridge lands — `vendor/zenoh-pico` submodule + `crates/zenoh-pico-sys` cmake+bindgen crate. First FFI call from wz Rust into zenoh-pico C library succeeds (4/4 smoke tests on `_z_id_len`). Layer 3 wire-interop foundation in place; R42 can now construct byte-equivalence assertions between wz-emit and zenoh-pico-emit for the same logical message.
changes_bullets: vendor/zenoh-pico submodule added (eclipse-zenoh/zenoh-pico) pinned at 3b3ab65c (1.9.0+10 commits). Matches the R5 zenoh-pico reference state used throughout R29+ SCXML authoring evidence chain. .gitmodules updated.

crates/zenoh-pico-sys/ new crate (Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/lib.rs + tests/ffi_smoke.rs). Build-deps: cmake 0.1 + bindgen 0.71. Crate-level `links = "zenohpico"` declares the static library to cargo's link DAG.

build.rs two-stage: (1) cmake::Config invokes vendor/zenoh-pico/CMakeLists.txt with BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF + minimum-features flag set, produces libzenohpico.a under OUT_DIR/lib/; (2) bindgen parses include/zenoh-pico.h with allowlisted symbols only (`_z_id_t` + `_z_id_len` for R41 smoke). Linux/macOS platform macro (`ZENOH_LINUX` / `ZENOH_MACOS`) derived from cargo TARGET env var — zenoh-pico's platform.h errors out on missing macro at clang parse time.

src/lib.rs `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/bindings.rs"))` exposes the FFI surface with the bindgen-required `#![allow(non_*case*)]` set. Documented as raw FFI — NOT a safe wrapper. Layer 3 test harness (R42+) is the intended consumer.

tests/ffi_smoke.rs (4 tests): _z_id_len exercised across all four `_z_id_t.id[16]` topologies (all-zero / partial-trailing-zeros / all-non-zero / mid-buffer-trailing-nonzero). Each asserts the C function's strip-trailing-zeros loop behavior matches expected length, proving the FFI bridge transfers struct-by-value correctly across the C ABI boundary.
crates/Cargo.toml workspace members extended to include zenoh-pico-sys.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p zenoh-pico-sys: pass. CMake configures + builds + installs libzenohpico.a (with all upstream platform support layers compiled), bindgen emits bindings.rs into OUT_DIR, rustc links the static lib into the test binary. ~30s cold-build, sub-second warm.
cargo test -p zenoh-pico-sys: 4/4 passed / 0 failed. All four _z_id_len cases (lengths 0/5/16/10) match the expected output of zenoh-pico's strip-trailing-zeros loop at src/protocol/core.c:35-45.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 40→41, T3 warn 856 unchanged.

FFI integrity proven empirically: (a) bindgen-emitted `_z_id_t` struct layout matches C's `struct { uint8_t id[16] }` (4 different non-zero patterns transfer correctly); (b) static linker resolves _z_id_len from libzenohpico.a; (c) C function called by Rust returns correct uint8_t values across the FFI boundary.
carry_forward_bullets: R42 = `crates/wz-integration-tests` Layer 3 wire-interop tests. Expand zenoh-pico-sys allowlist to include `_z_keep_alive_encode` (simplest body, single MID + 0 body bytes — matches the keep_alive.scxml codec wz emit). Then expand to msg_put / msg_del / scout / hello / etc. Each new codec verified = one new allowlist entry + one new test. Per-codec round-trip: (a) construct a known logical message, (b) encode via wz_codecs::X::encode(), (c) encode via zenoh-pico's _z_*_encode FFI, (d) byte-compare. If they differ, the wz codec SCXML's spec mapping has a bug — first REAL spec validation.

SCE upstream PR carry (R40) still pending: codegen template `#![doc]` inner-attribute fix in `tools/codegen/templates/forge/rust/<kind>.rs.jinja2`. Drop-in fix when SCE ships it; until then wz-codecs/build.rs's strip workaround stays.
R43+ = expand wz-codecs to wire all 21 codec SCXMLs (currently 8 of 21). Each new codec lands a paired Layer 3 test in wz-integration-tests.
R44+ = `sce-codegen build deploy/ap_standalone.yaml` end-to-end exercise. Validates the deploy-driven build pipeline produces a coherent `out/ap/` Cargo crate consumable by AP runtimes.

R45+ = `crates/wz-runtime-tokio` (sce_link_runtime_tokio impl per docs/runtime-crate-tokio.md). Session FSM script-placeholder wiring round (R29 carry) becomes viable once the runtime trait surface lands.

Phase 4 CI (R46+): GitHub Actions running validate_workspace + verify-codegen.sh + cargo test (all crates) + Layer 3 interop. The Layer 3 lane is what makes the CI gate meaningful — prior to R42, CI would only catch codegen regressions, not wire-format divergence.

Round 42.decision_summary: Layer 3 wire-interop verification lands. First objective evidence in 42 rounds that a watching-zenoh codec SCXML is byte-equivalent to zenoh-pico 1.9.0's wire encoding: close codec emits identical bytes to _z_close_encode across 10 reason-byte test cases. The spec is no longer just an internally-consistent artifact — it is wire-format-validated against the canonical reference implementation for the first codec.
changes_bullets: wz-codecs/build.rs CODECS array extended with close + frame + fragment. wz-codecs/src/lib.rs gains matching pub mod declarations. cargo build pass.

zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h new — bindgen umbrella that pulls in zenoh-pico.h PLUS internal protocol/iobuf.h + protocol/codec/transport.h + protocol/codec/message.h. Layer 3 needs the internal _z_*_encode surface which is intentionally not transitively included by the public umbrella.

zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist expanded with _z_t_msg_close_t + _z_wbuf_t + _z_zbuf_t + _z_close_encode + _z_wbuf_make + _z_wbuf_len + _z_wbuf_clear + _z_wbuf_to_zbuf + _z_zbuf_clear. Each entry justified by the close Layer 3 test.
crates/wz-integration-tests/ new crate — Phase 2 Layer 3 wire-interop test host. dev-deps wz-codecs + zenoh-pico-sys + sce-forge-runtime.
tests/layer3_close.rs (110 lines, 2 tests): byte-compare across 10 reason values + wire-shape pin (1-byte body invariant). FFI byte extraction via wbuf -> zbuf -> _ios._buf[.._w_pos].
crates/Cargo.toml workspace members extended (wz-integration-tests added).
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: 8/8 pass / 0 fail. wz-codecs smoke 2/2, zenoh-pico-sys ffi_smoke 4/4, wz-integration-tests layer3_close 2/2.

Layer 3 close exercises the FULL FFI pipeline: bindgen-emitted _z_t_msg_close_t constructed in Rust; _z_close_encode called across C ABI; bytes extracted from _z_wbuf_t via _z_wbuf_to_zbuf + _z_iosli_t._buf[.._w_pos]; resources cleaned via _z_zbuf_clear + _z_wbuf_clear.

Byte-equivalence: Close { reason }.encode() (wz) == _z_close_encode(wbf, header, &_z_t_msg_close_t { _reason: reason }) bytes (zenoh-pico) for every reason in {0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04, 0x05, 0x06, 0x07, 0x42, 0xFF}. 10/10 match.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 41 to 42, T3 warn 856 unchanged.

Historical significance: 42 rounds (R1-R41) authored a codec spec WITHOUT wire-format validation. R42 supplies the first such validation — the close codec SCXML correctly models zenoh-pico's _z_close_encode output.
carry_forward_bullets: R43 = Layer 3 expansion to frame + fragment codecs. Both are §4.2 data-carrying with VLE sn + tail payload. Adds _z_frame_encode + _z_fragment_encode to zenoh-pico-sys allowlist. First VLE wire-byte agreement validation — VLE u64 is the most pervasive primitive across the codec set.
R44 = Layer 3 across handshake bodies: init_body + open_body + join + scout + hello. Exercises multi-bit flag, dotted-path length-ref, present-if local + parent.X, repeat, import primitives.
R45 = Layer 3 across payload bodies: msg_put + msg_del + ext_entry + child codecs (timestamp / encoding / ext_unit/zint/zbuf). Variant + embed + tlv-chain + entry-flag termination validated.
R46+ = network envelopes (request / response_final), declare bodies, OAM. After this the §3-§7 codec catalog is fully Layer 3-verified against zenoh-pico 1.9.0.

SCE upstream PR carry (R40) remains: #![doc = SCE-MAP] inner-attr template fix in tools/codegen/templates/forge/rust/<kind>.rs.jinja2. Drop-in fix when SCE ships it; wz-codecs/build.rs strip workaround stays in the meantime.

Round 43.decision_summary: Layer 3 expansion — fragment + frame codecs validated against zenoh-pico 1.9.0 wire format across VLE width boundaries (1/2/3+ byte forms). VLE u64 primitive byte-correctness now empirically verified; downstream codecs that consume VLE (sn, lease, batch_size, payload_len, ~20 others) inherit confidence transitively. Cross-codec frame↔fragment body-equivalence pinned.
changes_bullets: zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist gains: _z_t_msg_frame_t + _z_t_msg_fragment_t + _z_slice_t + _z_delete_context_t + _z_frame_encode + _z_fragment_encode + _z_slice_as_zbuf. All justified by R43 Layer 3 tests.

tests/layer3_fragment.rs (130 lines, 2 tests): VLE width boundary corpus (sn=0/1/127/128/256/16383/16384/1M/u32::MAX) × payload variants (empty / 1-byte / multi-byte). Non-owning _z_slice_t wrap of Rust &[u8]; empty payload skipped via NULL start pointer + _z_slice_check short-circuit.

tests/layer3_frame.rs (130 lines, 2 tests): same VLE corpus via the frame path. Construction shape differs from fragment — _z_t_msg_frame_t._payload is *mut _z_zbuf_t (NOT _z_slice_t by value); zbuf built via _z_slice_as_zbuf wrapping a Rust slice, stored in MaybeUninit to avoid unused_assignments lint on the empty-payload null branch. Empty payload: _payload=null short-circuit per upstream code.

Cross-codec frame↔fragment body-equivalence: when header=0 (no Z flag, no first/drop extensions), frame and fragment produce identical body bytes for the same (sn, payload) input. The transport-layer MID distinction (T_FRAME 0x05 vs T_FRAGMENT 0x06) is written by the envelope encoder, not the body codecs.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: 12/12 pass / 0 fail. Breakdown by test target: wz-codecs smoke 2/2 (R40), zenoh-pico-sys ffi_smoke 4/4 (R41), wz-integration-tests layer3_close 2/2 (R42), layer3_fragment 2/2 (R43 NEW), layer3_frame 2/2 (R43 NEW).

VLE u64 primitive validated across width boundaries: 1-byte VLE (sn=0/1/127), 2-byte VLE (sn=128/256/16383), 3-byte VLE (sn=16384/1M), 4+-byte VLE (sn=u32::MAX). Both encoders produce identical byte sequences at every boundary — the most pervasive codec primitive (used by ~20 wire fields across the catalog) has now passed objective Layer 3 verification.

Cross-codec invariant pinned: wz_codecs::frame::Frame { sn, payload }.encode() == wz_codecs::fragment::Fragment { sn, payload }.encode() when extensions are off (header=0). Caught at test time if the codec SCXMLs ever diverge on the shared §4.2 body shape.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 42→43, T3 warn 856 unchanged.

Cumulative Layer 3 coverage at end of R43: close + fragment + frame = 3 of 18 wz-codecs SCXMLs wire-validated. Each remaining codec (15 more) lands its own layer3_<codec>.rs in subsequent rounds with the same byte-compare pattern.
carry_forward_bullets: R44 = Layer 3 across handshake bodies (init_body + open_body + join + scout + hello). R45 = Layer 3 across payload bodies (msg_put + msg_del). Exercises variant (ext_entry inner dispatch), embed (timestamp + encoding), tlv-chain (ext_entry repeat), entry-flag termination. Heaviest primitive load.
R46 = Layer 3 across network envelopes (request + response_final). Exercises peek-byte primitive.
R47 = remaining codecs: locator (string), timestamp + encoding (embeddable), ext_unit/zint/zbuf (variant arms). After R47 the entire 18-codec wz-codecs catalog has Layer 3 verification.

Round 44.decision_summary: Layer 3 handshake codec expansion — scout / open_body / init_body / join validated. **Layer 3 caught the first real spec drift**: SCE codec_zenoh_init_body + codec_zenoh_join wrongly inherited `sce:default-endian="big"` while zenoh-pico's `_z_uint16_encode` is LE (LSB-first). wz init_body + join SCXMLs corrected to `default-endian="little"`; SCE fixture upstream PR carried for matching fix. Production-grade validation now empirically catching incorrect spec assumptions.
changes_bullets: wz-codecs/build.rs CODECS array gains scout + init_body + open_body + join. lib.rs gains 4 matching pub mod declarations. cargo build pass.
zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist expanded: _z_scout_encode + _z_init_encode + _z_open_encode + _z_join_encode + their msg structs + z_what_t + z_whatami_t.

zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h gains forward declarations for _z_scout_encode + _z_hello_encode (file-private in zenoh-pico's message.c, no header decl upstream). Symbols already in libzenohpico.a; declaration only changes bindgen + linker visibility.
tests/layer3_scout.rs (110 lines, 3 tests) — cbyte multi-bit pack + local present-if gate (zid emission on cbyte.I). Validated with 0/3/16-byte zid corpus.
tests/layer3_open_body.rs (110 lines, 3 tests) — parent.A NEGATION gate (B5-λ). Cookie present iff parent.A=0; VLE width boundary corpus for lease + initial_sn.
tests/layer3_init_body.rs (190 lines, 4 tests) — parent.S + parent.A positive gates. **CAUGHT SPEC DRIFT**: wz BE batch_size diverged from zenoh-pico LE. Fix applied + audit-traced.
tests/layer3_join.rs (185 lines, 3 tests) — parent.S gate + multi-VLE next_sn chain (3 VLE u64 fields). Same LE batch_size fix as init_body.

changes_bullets: .github/workflows/ci.yml new (~150 lines). Single ubuntu-22.04 runner job with: checkout (submodules: recursive) + apt cmake/libclang/clang/perl + rust toolchain (stable, with rustfmt+clippy) + cache (Swatinem/rust-cache v2 for crates + vendor/sce) + mnemosyne-cli install (graceful skip on failure) + build-sce.sh + mnemosyne validate-workspace + per-codec verify-codegen.sh sweep with audit-traced LE-divergence allowlist for init_body+join + cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.

wz-codecs/src/lib.rs gains crate-level `#![allow(clippy::all)]` — the entire crate body is sce-codegen include!()'d output, so clippy style lints are SCE template authoring concerns not consumer-tunable. Workspace rustc `warnings = "deny"` policy stays in force (rustc warnings ARE consumer-actionable).
zenoh-pico-sys/src/lib.rs gains `#![allow(clippy::all)]` for the same reason — entire crate is bindgen FFI output.

wz-integration-tests test files cleaned: `manual_range_contains` (3 places) — `zid_len >= 1 && zid_len <= 16` → `(1..=16).contains(&zid_len)`; `unnecessary identity map` (msg_put: `(0..255u8).map(|i| i).collect()` → `.collect()`); doc list indentation in fragment + init_body. All clippy lints in human-authored code resolved at source, not muted.
verification_bullets: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean (0 warnings, 0 errors).
cargo test --workspace: 32/32 pass (no regression from clippy fixes).

changes_bullets: SCE upstream finding: sce-codegen has NO `build` subcommand at vendor pin 11f1032d. R50 verified the absence empirically (`sce-codegen --help` lists only generate/orchestrate/...). The full deploy build is gated on SCE upstream implementing the subcommand.

scripts/validate-deploy.sh new (~80 lines). For each deploy/*.yaml: (1) YAML parses; (2) top-level `machines` key present + non-empty; (3) each machine declares `platform.class` + `platform.os`. python3+yaml stdlib used — zero external Rust deps. Holding-pattern gate documented in script header; upgrade path when SCE ships `build`.

Round 51.decision_summary: Phase Z entry — FIRST OBSERVABLE COMMUNICATION milestone reached. wz-runtime-tokio crate lands with LinkDriver trait + TcpDriver impl + echo_demo integration test. Two tokio tasks on a single process exchange a real wz-codecs-encoded MsgPut body over TCP loopback; subscriber decodes back to a structurally-equivalent MsgPut. The R28 user question "when will we see communication?" is concretely answered at R51.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/ new crate (3 files): Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs + tests/echo_demo.rs. Workspace member added.

src/lib.rs: LinkDriver trait (4 methods open/send/close/poll_event matching docs/runtime-crate-tokio.md §2.1 baseline) + TxFrame/RxFrame/LinkEvent/Reliability enums + TcpDriver impl (4-byte BE length-prefixed framing over TcpStream).

tests/echo_demo.rs: publisher + subscriber tokio tasks on loopback TCP. Publisher: MsgPut header=0x01 + payload=[0xCA,0xFE,0xBA,0xBE,0x42] encoded via wz-codecs, sent via TcpDriver. Subscriber: accept + poll_event Rx + decode via wz-codecs::msg_put::MsgPut::decode + assert equality. PASS.
crates/Cargo.toml: members extends to include wz-runtime-tokio.
Workspace: 33/33 tests pass + clippy --deny warnings clean across all 4 crates.
verification_bullets: R28 question answered: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test echo_demo passes. MsgPut wire bytes flow through TCP loopback and decode back successfully.
cargo test --workspace: 33/33 pass / 0 fail. +1 echo_demo vs R47.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean.

What this proves: encode↔decode self-consistency + Layer 3 zenoh-pico byte-equivalence are not just in-memory properties — they survive a real TCP socket boundary. The bytes Layer 3-validated against zenoh-pico are the SAME bytes flowing on the wire.
What this does NOT yet prove: zenoh-pico endpoint interop (needs session-FSM handshake — R54); multi-message + reliability + extensions; framing-error paths.

R54 = session_fsm_unicast R29 script-action placeholder wiring (link_driver_open / send_init_syn / etc. → wz-runtime-tokio actual calls). After R54 the FSM drives encoded handshake bytes on a real TCP link — zenoh-pico endpoint interop becomes possible.

What this proves: encode↔decode self-consistency + Layer 3 zenoh-pico byte-equivalence are not just in-memory properties — they survive a real TCP socket boundary. The bytes Layer 3-validated against zenoh-pico are the SAME bytes flowing on the wire.
What this does NOT yet prove: zenoh-pico endpoint interop (needs session-FSM handshake — R54); multi-message + reliability + extensions; framing-error paths.

Round 52.decision_summary: Echo demo expansion — UdpDriver impl added + msg_del/TCP + msg_put/UDP round-trip tests. Phase Z reaches 35 workspace tests. Both push-body codec variants (msg_put + msg_del) now demonstrably survive a real network boundary across BOTH transport classes (TCP + UDP). Reliability::BestEffort hint wired (UDP semantic).
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs: UdpDriver impl added. from_socket(socket, peer) constructor + open/send/close/poll_event implementations. Honors Reliability::BestEffort silently (UDP is inherently best-effort); Reliability::Reliable hint is forwarded but UDP can't enforce — the session FSM's responsibility per docs/runtime-crate-tokio.md §6.

tests/echo_demo_del_and_udp.rs (130 lines, 2 tests). echo_msg_del_tcp_round_trip: MsgDel header=0x02 + no flags + no payload across TCP loopback. echo_msg_put_udp_round_trip: MsgPut header=0x01 + 3-byte payload across UDP datagram — datagram preserves message boundary so no 4-byte length prefix needed.
Both codec variants of the §6.1 PushBody family validated end-to-end through both link transport classes the project supports for AP linux.

build.rs invokes sce-build's compile_forge_with_imports(Language::C11) in-process; emitted lwip_udp_scout.h composed with cc-compiled sce_link_runtime_lwip.c (vtable skeleton — host-build NOP stubs counting rx/tx/poll calls).

Discarded an earlier hand-authored sce_link_runtime.h drafted against docs/runtime-crate-lwip.md §2 spec when the user flagged that the B6 emitter was already live; reframed R53 to consume the canonical emit instead of hand-locking a divergent contract.
doc §6 atomic intent updated via set_section_intent to retire the 'design-only until C11 emitter exists' stale claim and record the emitter-live + skeleton-landed state.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean; cargo test --workspace 39/39 pass (35 baseline + 4 new wz-runtime-lwip ABI smoke tests).

cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

carry_forward_bullets: R54 = R29 session_fsm script-action placeholder wiring to wz-runtime-tokio LinkDriver (23-round stale carry; final Phase Z closure).

R53b = docs/runtime-crate-lwip.md §2 spec divergence — hand-authored 6-event enum + 3-outbound function contract differs from B6 emitter's 3-function vtable shape. Decide whether §2 prose is rewritten to match the emitter or retained as a higher-level session-layer contract above the B6 driver vtable.

R40 carry reframe — SCE-side fix is not the path: sce_map_marker.jinja2 emits BOTH `#![doc=SCE-MAP]` and `// SCE-MAP` per RFC §5.O spec lines 3135-3136 'MUST emit BOTH' obligation. The include!() consumption pattern in wz-codecs is the incompatibility surface; options are (a) keep build.rs strip workaround, (b) switch to per-codec sub-crates, (c) SCE PR adding `--no-inner-attr` carve-out.

R44 carry still open — vendor/sce/tests/forge/resources/codec_zenoh_init_body.scxml + codec_zenoh_join.scxml retain sce:default-endian='big'; no upstream change between 11f1032d and 2ff9d7a9 touching those files.
R50 carry still open — sce-codegen lacks a `build <deploy.yaml>` subcommand; the new `orchestrate` subcommand is a weaker multi-doc form that does not consume deploy.yaml.

In-process API anchor gap — sce-build's compile_forge_with_imports omits the SCE-GENERATED + source-hash + template-hash + generated-at 4-line block that the CLI binary emits, so wz build.rs outputs (wz-codecs, wz-runtime-lwip) lack RFC §5.O traceability anchors entirely. Separate from the CLI workspace-root fix; needs an in-process anchor-emit pass.
53 + R53 commits unpushed; pre-push hook re-runs validate_workspace + commit-msg formatter at push time. Push remains explicit-consent only.

Round 54.decision_summary: R29 session_fsm.scxml script-action placeholders wired to wz-runtime-tokio LinkDriver via sce-rust-lua + register_global_function — 23-round stale carry resolved.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml: sce-rust-runtime + sce-rust-lua + log + sce-build (build-dep) added; SCE statechart-kind codegen + Lua script engine pulled into the workspace's dep graph.

wz-runtime-tokio/build.rs: shells out to `vendor/sce/target/release/sce-codegen generate --language rust` with `--workspace-root` plus a post-emit strip pass that removes both `#![...]` attr-style inner attributes and `//!` inner doc comments (extends the R40 carry strip to statechart emit; lib.rs's wrapping module restores the lint allows as OUTER attributes).
wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs: `pub mod session_fsm_unicast` exposes the generated emit; `pub mod session_glue` carries the new SessionLinkActions + 17-fn install surface.

wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs (~300 lines): SessionLinkActions struct owns BoxedLinkDriver + ActionTrace; install_session_actions registers 17 native Rust closures with the process-wide Lua engine (sce_rust_lua::lua_engine_singleton + register_global_function) — 7 outbound link calls (link_driver_open/send_init_syn/send_open_syn/send_init_ack_with_cookie/send_open_ack/send_close_frame_with_reason/release_link), 6 state-internal actions (enable_rx_tx_regions, start/stop_lease_monitor, start/stop_keepalive_worker, free_pool_slots), 4 close-reason discriminator setters, and 3 boolean guards (half_open_cap_available/accept_rate_token/cookie_valid).

TokioLinkDriverAdapter<D: LinkDriver> implements BoxedLinkDriver by holding a tokio::runtime::Handle and `block_on`-ing the async LinkDriver methods inside the sync NativeMethod closure shape (Lua's register_global_function takes a `Fn(&[ScriptValue]) -> ScriptValue + Send + Sync`).

wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_glue_dispatch.rs (~140 lines): single integration test walking the full happy-path + listener + close-reason script-action sequence through `dispatch_script`, asserting both the trace counters on SessionLinkActions and the RecordingDriver-side open/close/sends list. Single test on purpose — the Lua engine singleton + register_global_function are process-global, so cargo test's thread-parallel runner races concurrent installs.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean; cargo test --workspace 39 → 40 (one new R54 integration test).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (one doc_lazy_continuation cleanup in lib.rs module-level doc).
validate_workspace post-mutation: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 54 → 55 (after this Round 54 append), sections 214 unchanged.

Recording driver verified 1 open + 1 close + 5 outbound sends (INIT_SYN / OPEN_SYN / CLOSE / INIT_ACK_COOKIE / OPEN_ACK), each marked Reliability::Reliable, in source-order matching the SCXML's onentry script ordering across the initiator + listener handshake paths.
carry_forward_bullets: R55 = codec-encoded wire bytes for the 7 outbound link calls. Current placeholders (b'INIT_SYN', b'OPEN_SYN', etc.) sit where the proper init_body/open_body/close codec emit + transport-message header + cookie payload bytes will land. Splits cleanly from R54 because the wire-format choices need session-layer transport-header decisions that belong in their own round.

R55b = wire the Engine-driving path. R54 verified the script-action dispatch in isolation (`dispatch_script(name)` direct call); the next step is to construct `Engine::new(SessionFsmUnicastPolicy::new())`, `raise_external_by_name("outbound.start", "")`, `step()` quiescence, and assert the Engine's emitted execute_script sequence matches the action-ordering R54 pinned by hand.

Lua-engine process-globality is a production concern for multi-peer FSM instances on one process. Two FSM instances would race on `register_global_function` name space (link_driver_open() means whose LinkDriver). Either (a) push per-session bindings via `bind_native_object(session_id, ...)` and use session-scoped function tables, or (b) accept the single-FSM-per-process constraint and document it. Decide before multi-peer integration tests land.

R29 SCXML script-name single-source-of-truth audit. R54 wired 17 native fns by string name match; if `session_fsm_unicast.scxml` adds a new <script>foo()</script> without a matching install_session_actions registration, the Lua engine silently returns Nil + the SCE FSM continues. Consider a SCE codegen pass that emits the action-name set as a const `&[&str]` and a build-time check that `register_global_function` covers every name in that set.

R40 carry strip widened to `#![...]` + `//!` lines — the R40 SCE PR option (`--no-inner-attr` carve-out) would now also need to suppress module-level `//!` doc; rethink the SCE PR shape or accept the wz-side strip as the durable answer.
53 + R53 + R54 commits unpushed; pre-push hook re-runs validate_workspace at push time. Push remains explicit-consent only.

R54 verified the script-action dispatch in isolation (`dispatch_script(name)` direct call); the next step is to construct `Engine::new(SessionFsmUnicastPolicy::new())`, `raise_external_by_name("outbound.start", "")`, `step()` quiescence, and assert the Engine's emitted execute_script sequence matches the action-ordering R54 pinned by hand.

Lua-engine process-globality is a production concern for multi-peer FSM instances on one process. Two FSM instances would race on `register_global_function` name space (link_driver_open() means whose LinkDriver). Either (a) push per-session bindings via `bind_native_object(session_id, ...)` and use session-scoped function tables, or (b) accept the single-FSM-per-process constraint and document it. Decide before multi-peer integration tests land.

R29 SCXML script-name single-source-of-truth audit. R54 wired 17 native fns by string name match; if `session_fsm_unicast.scxml` adds a new <script>foo()</script> without a matching install_session_actions registration, the Lua engine silently returns Nil + the SCE FSM continues. Consider a SCE codegen pass that emits the action-name set as a const `&[&str]` and a build-time check that `register_global_function` covers every name in that set.

R40 carry strip widened to `#![...]` + `//!` lines — the R40 SCE PR option (`--no-inner-attr` carve-out) would now also need to suppress module-level `//!` doc; rethink the SCE PR shape or accept the wz-side strip as the durable answer.
53 + R53 + R54 commits unpushed; pre-push hook re-runs validate_workspace at push time. Push remains explicit-consent only.

Round 57.decision_summary: Wire-format reality + production safety guards — placeholder bytes swapped to real codec encoding; single-install + multi-thread + script-name SSoT guards land in one pass.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs (~280 lines rewrite): SessionInitParams struct (version/whatami/zid/seq_num_res/req_id_res/batch_size/lease/lease_in_seconds/initial_sn/cookie); encode_init / encode_open / encode_close compose wz-codecs::{init_body,open_body,close}::encode (Layer 3 byte-equiv verified vs zenoh-pico _z_*_encode) + transport-message header byte (T_MID + parent flags from vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/definitions/transport.h).

Placeholder bytes b"INIT_SYN" / b"OPEN_SYN" / b"CLOSE" / b"INIT_ACK_COOKIE" / b"OPEN_ACK" replaced with real codec output. CloseReason discriminator now flows from the trace into encode_close (reason byte sourced from the session.close transition that set the reason).

INSTALLED: OnceLock<Arc<SessionLinkActions>> single-install guard; install_session_actions returns Err(SessionActionsAlreadyInstalled) on the second call per process (closes R54+R55b carry on multi-FSM Lua global namespace race).
TokioLinkDriverAdapter::new asserts runtime_flavor == MultiThread at construction site (closes the R54 carry on block_on deadlock risk; converts a silent runtime hazard into a fail-fast panic).

wz-runtime-tokio/build.rs (audit_script_names + find_script_names + find_cond_names): build-time audit confirms every <script>foo()</script> body + cond= identifier in sources/session/*.scxml matches the EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES set. Mismatch panics the build (closes R54 carry on silent Nil from un-registered script names).

rebind_session_actions_for_test(actions) exposed (#[doc(hidden)]) so cargo test's thread-parallel runner with the process-global INSTALLED OnceLock can swap closures inside a single test binary without violating the production single-install invariant.

tests/session_glue_dispatch.rs rewritten — every outbound's wire bytes asserted byte-exact against an expected sequence computed independently from the fixture inputs (no shared helper) so an implementation drift surfaces as a mismatch.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean post-rewrite.
cargo test --workspace: 41 → 43 (R57 inline double-install assertion + R57 init_cbyte/pack_sn_res unit tests).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (derivable-impls clippy lint fixed by switching CloseReason to #[derive(Default)] + #[default]).
validate_workspace post-mutation: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 57 → 58 (after this Round 57 append).

Wire-byte assertions confirm: send_init_syn = [FLAG_S|T_MID_INIT, version, cbyte, zid..., sn_res, batch_size_lo, batch_size_hi]; send_open_syn body carries cookie payload, OpenAck body does NOT; CLOSE = [FLAG_T_CLOSE_S|T_MID_CLOSE, reason_byte]; InitAck flags = FLAG_S|FLAG_A; OpenAck flags = FLAG_T|FLAG_A.
Production-safety guards exercised in tests: inline double-install in r57 test asserts Err(SessionActionsAlreadyInstalled); script-name audit runs on every build.
carry_forward_bullets: R57c = R55b Engine-driving extended to full happy-path coverage. Currently only Init → LinkOpening verified. Engine::tick or external event injection (link.opened, init_ack.received, open_ack.received) drives the full 4-way handshake; Established / Closing / Closed terminal states still untested at FSM level.

R57b = cookie generation via HMAC-SHA256. SessionInitParams::cookie is currently caller-supplied bytes; production InitAck side must HMAC-SHA256(per-process-secret, peer_zid || nonce) and OpenSyn side echoes the received cookie. Wires to sce_intrinsics_runtime::hmac_sha256 (deferred RFC §5.M intrinsic).

R57d = batch_size wire shape audit. wz init_body encode emits batch_size as 2 bytes little-endian; transport.h documents it as u16. The Layer 3 layer3_init_body_s1_a1 fixture passes for both, but real zenoh-pico decode behaviour with a VLE-encoded batch_size variant (if any future patch level introduces one) needs cross-check.

changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_full_path.rs (~180 lines): R59 integration test walks the entire outbound-initiator state path via Engine::process_event(LinkOpened/InitAckReceived/OpenAckReceived/SessionClose/ClosingTimeout) and asserts at every state: (a) `Engine::get_current_state` matches the SCXML target, (b) the onentry/onexit script counters in ActionTrace increment by exactly one each, (c) the RecordingDriver receives the expected open/close/send calls, (d) the Close wire bytes match the codec-produced 2-byte layout [FLAG_T_CLOSE_S|T_MID_CLOSE, reason].

Closes R57c carry (Engine multi-step driving past LinkOpening) using external-event injection — the 5s link.open_timeout / 100ms closing.timeout delayed sends scheduled inside onentry are not advanced because the happy path drives transitions via real wire events (link.opened / closing.timeout) rather than timeouts. Engine::tick + Hal time mock remains a separate carry for the timeout/failure-path coverage.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 43 → 44 (R59 new integration test); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

Engine state assertion at every transition target (Init/LinkOpening/SentInitSyn/GotInitAck/Established/Closing/Closed) confirms the generated SCXML transition graph traverses exactly one macrostep per process_event call.

Established onentry produces 3 actions (enable_rx_tx_regions, start_lease_monitor, start_keepalive_worker); Established onexit produces 2 (stop_keepalive_worker, stop_lease_monitor); test asserts every counter +1 in the right order.

Wire bytes for INIT_SYN + OPEN_SYN + CLOSE in the RecordingDriver sends queue confirm the R57 codec path is exercised through the Engine driver, not just the dispatch_script direct call from R57's test.
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 59 → 60.
carry_forward_bullets: Failure-path coverage: framing.error / lease.expired / tx.congestion.exhaust / peer.close / link.lost / init_ack.timeout / open_ack.timeout transitions still untested at FSM level. Each fires set_close_reason_* + transitions to Closing; the wire shape is identical to the happy-path Close so the codec assertion stays unchanged.

Engine::tick + Hal time mock: the 5s link.open_timeout / 2s init_ack.timeout / 100ms closing.timeout / accepting.inactivity_timeout delayed sends are scheduled but never advanced. Production timeout coverage needs `sce_rust_runtime::StdHal` injection or a TestHal that advances logical time.

Listener-path coverage: R59 walks the initiator path (outbound.start → LinkOpening → Opening). The Accepting branch (inbound.start → AwaitingInitSyn → SentInitAck → SentOpenAck → Established) exercises send_init_ack_with_cookie + send_open_ack which R57 wire tests cover in isolation, but Engine-driven listener-path is its own follow-up test.

Round 59.publishable_decision_summary: Engine multi-step driving — full outbound-initiator happy path Init→LinkOpening→SentInitSyn→GotInitAck→Established→Closing→Closed verified through real codec encode.
publishable_changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_full_path.rs (~180 lines): R59 integration test walks the entire outbound-initiator state path via Engine::process_event(LinkOpened/InitAckReceived/OpenAckReceived/SessionClose/ClosingTimeout) and asserts at every state: (a) `Engine::get_current_state` matches the SCXML target, (b) the onentry/onexit script counters in ActionTrace increment by exactly one each, (c) the RecordingDriver receives the expected open/close/send calls, (d) the Close wire bytes match the codec-produced 2-byte layout [FLAG_T_CLOSE_S|T_MID_CLOSE, reason].

Closes R57c carry (Engine multi-step driving past LinkOpening) using external-event injection — the 5s link.open_timeout / 100ms closing.timeout delayed sends scheduled inside onentry are not advanced because the happy path drives transitions via real wire events (link.opened / closing.timeout) rather than timeouts. Engine::tick + Hal time mock remains a separate carry for the timeout/failure-path coverage.
publishable_verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 43 → 44 (R59 new integration test); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

Engine state assertion at every transition target (Init/LinkOpening/SentInitSyn/GotInitAck/Established/Closing/Closed) confirms the generated SCXML transition graph traverses exactly one macrostep per process_event call.

Established onentry produces 3 actions (enable_rx_tx_regions, start_lease_monitor, start_keepalive_worker); Established onexit produces 2 (stop_keepalive_worker, stop_lease_monitor); test asserts every counter +1 in the right order.

Wire bytes for INIT_SYN + OPEN_SYN + CLOSE in the RecordingDriver sends queue confirm the R57 codec path is exercised through the Engine driver, not just the dispatch_script direct call from R57's test.
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 59 → 60.

Each fires set_close_reason_* + transitions to Closing; the wire shape is identical to the happy-path Close so the codec assertion stays unchanged.

Engine::tick + Hal time mock: the 5s link.open_timeout / 2s init_ack.timeout / 100ms closing.timeout / accepting.inactivity_timeout delayed sends are scheduled but never advanced. Production timeout coverage needs `sce_rust_runtime::StdHal` injection or a TestHal that advances logical time.

Listener-path coverage: R59 walks the initiator path (outbound.start → LinkOpening → Opening). Round 60.decision_summary: Strict audit pass — encapsulation hardening closes 6 R57/R58/R59 self-review defects: dead code, public test surface, default misuse, silent failures, list duplication, weak invariants.
changes_bullets: SessionInitParams::default removed. New SessionInitParams::for_test() helper gated behind `_test_support` Cargo feature so production builds cannot construct a SessionInitParams via Default (consumer would have silently shipped fixed test bytes on the wire).

rebind_session_actions_for_test + dispatch_script gated behind `_test_support`. Public surface in production builds excludes both. wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml grew a [features] section with `default = []` and `_test_support = []`; tests opt in via a self-referencing dev-dependency.

dispatch_script defensive: debug_assert that the requested name is in REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES. Even with `_test_support` enabled, a caller cannot smuggle arbitrary Lua source — only the production registration set is dispatchable.

bind_unit / bind_close_reason / bind_bool: debug_assert!(ok, ...) replaced with assert!(ok, ...). register_global_function failure now panics in release builds too; the silent-failure window of debug_assert (release-build skip) is gone.

REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES doc-comment promoted to single-source-of-truth marker. EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES (build.rs) hand-mirror retained but the consolidation rationale is recorded so future divergence is caught at audit.

tests/session_fsm_real_tcp.rs (~120 lines): tokio multi-thread test wires TcpListener loopback pair, plumbs `TokioLinkDriverAdapter<TcpDriver>` into `SessionLinkActions`, drives Engine through Init→LinkOpening→SentInitSyn, then reads the wire bytes off the peer socket. First end-to-end proof of the production chain (Engine → Lua → SessionLinkActions → adapter → TcpDriver → kernel socket); closes the R57 self-review finding that TokioLinkDriverAdapter was dead code.
All R55b / R59 callers swapped to SessionInitParams::for_test (sed substitution across 3 test files).
let _ = sce_rust_lua::register() simplified — clippy::single_match fix; documented why the silent accept is sound (one-variant Result, AlreadyRegistered == engine already in place).
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 44 → 45 (R60 real-TCP integration); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

Peer-side read in r60 test confirms `payload[0] == 0x41` (FLAG_T_INIT_S | T_MID_INIT) + `payload[1] == 0x05` (version) actually traverse the full production stack onto a kernel socket. Any drift in Engine ↔ Lua ↔ adapter ↔ TcpDriver chain surfaces as a payload byte mismatch.

Production build with `--no-default-features` (no `_test_support`) does not expose `for_test`, `dispatch_script`, `rebind_session_actions_for_test`; the encapsulation guard is enforced by the compiler.
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 60 → 61.
carry_forward_bullets: R61 carry: failure-path Engine coverage (framing.error / lease.expired / tx.congestion.exhaust / peer.close / link.lost / *.timeout). Each fires set_close_reason_* + Closing branch; wire shape inherited from R57/R59 happy path.

R61 carry: listener-path Engine coverage (Accepting branch: inbound.start → AwaitingInitSyn → SentInitAck → SentOpenAck → Established). R57 dispatched send_init_ack_with_cookie + send_open_ack in isolation; Engine-driven listener walk pending.
R61 carry: Hal time mock for delayed `<send>` timeout coverage. sce_rust_runtime::StdHal injection or a TestHal that advances logical time.
R62 carry: cookie HMAC-SHA256 — SessionInitParams::cookie currently caller-supplied bytes. Production listener side must HMAC(secret, peer_zid || nonce) per RFC §5.M.
R62 carry: transport extension chain (§7 ext::QoS / ext::Auth / ext::MultiLink) emit on Init/Open. R57 emits base body only; real zenoh peer interop requires the extension chain.
R63 carry: wz-runtime-lwip resolution — doc-around-the-hack rejected at R58; either revert (preserve SCE B6 emitter audit via a Layer 3 test) or land actual lwIP API.
R40 long-term: SCE PR for `--no-inner-attr` flag or wz per-codec sub-crate split. R58 retained the strip workaround; the SCE-side fix is still the lower-cost path.
EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES (build.rs) and REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES (session_glue.rs) remain hand-maintained twins; consolidation via include!() or build artifact share is a refactor follow-up.

Round 61.decision_summary: Strict audit pass — full state-graph coverage. Every SCXML transition edge R59 happy-path missed is now Engine-driven and asserted.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_coverage.rs (~250 lines): single mega-test r61_full_coverage_sequential walks 11 scenarios covering every transition edge in session_fsm_unicast.scxml outside the R59 happy path — listener path (Init→AwaitingInitSyn→SentInitAck→SentOpenAck→Established), all 4 close-reason setters (Generic / Invalid / Expired / Unresponsive), all 5 close-from-state edges (LinkOpening / Opening.SentInitSyn / Opening / Established / Accepting), all 3 direct-to-Closed edges (link.lost from LinkOpening, peer.close from Established, link.lost from Accepting).

fresh_engine() helper isolates each scenario via rebind_session_actions_for_test (R60 feature-gated path); a fresh Driver + Actions triple resets every counter to zero between scenarios while INSTALLED OnceLock stays at its first-install pointer.

Single mega-test on purpose: cargo's thread-parallel runner races concurrent rebind_session_actions_for_test calls against the process-global Lua-engine global function namespace. Sequential dispatch in one test fn is the only race-safe shape until R60 carry on session-scoped Lua binding lands. Initially split into 11 #[test] fns and observed 9/11 races; consolidated to one fn after diagnosis.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 45 → 46 (R61 mega-test); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

11 SCXML transition edges verified end-to-end via Engine::process_event: listener path + LinkOpenTimeout/LinkLost/InitAckTimeout/FramingError(Opening)/OpenAckTimeout/LeaseExpired/FramingError(Established)/TxCongestionExhaust/PeerClose/FramingError(Accepting).

Each scenario asserts (a) Engine::get_current_state matches the SCXML target state, (b) trace.close_reason discriminator matches the set_close_reason_* action the transition runs, (c) send_close_frame_with_reason counter increments only on Closing-routed paths (not on direct-to-Closed via link.lost / peer.close).
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 61 → 62.
carry_forward_bullets: R61b carry: Hal time mock for delayed `<send>` timer fire (5s link.open_timeout / 2s init_ack.timeout / 2s open_ack.timeout / 100ms closing.timeout / accepting.inactivity_timeout). External event injection in R61 verifies the transition target + actions per timeout edge but does NOT exercise sce_rust_runtime's scheduler (PullScheduler + schedule_event_at / pop_ready_event_at). A TestHal with logical-time advance would close the gap; StdHal's `now_ticks_ms` is a free-function (associated, no &self) so a wz-side mock requires either feature-flag swap in sce_rust_runtime or a wrapper Engine layer.
R62 carry: cookie HMAC-SHA256 + transport extension chain (§7 ext::QoS / ext::Auth / ext::MultiLink) emit on Init/Open frames — still open from R60.
R63 carry: wz-runtime-lwip resolution — revert (Phase W defer) or land actual lwIP API; current state is R58 doc-comment reframe which the R57 self-review classified as document-around-the-hack.
R40 long-term: SCE PR for `--no-inner-attr` or wz per-codec sub-crate split.

Test-design carry: race-prone per-test #[test] fns documented as anti-pattern in R61 commentary; future test additions to wz-runtime-tokio should extend r61_full_coverage_sequential (or a new mega-test fn) rather than add isolated fns that the cargo runner parallelises.

Round 62.decision_summary: Strict audit pass — cookie HMAC-SHA256 primitive landed per RFC §5.M; extension chain (§7) carry deferred to R63 (SCXML revision scope).
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio depends on `hmac = 0.12` + `sha2 = 0.10` (RustCrypto). MCU sibling Phase W will swap for sce_intrinsics_runtime::hmac_sha256 once that intrinsic lands per RFC §5.I.

Public `generate_cookie_hmac_sha256(key, peer_zid) -> Vec<u8>` helper in session_glue. Output is HMAC-SHA256 truncated to 16 bytes (cookie wire width per RFC §5.M + zenoh-pico _z_t_msg_init_t._cookie ZSlice convention).
Short-key panic at construction site (>= 32 bytes per RFC §5.M). Test asserts the loud-reject path so a 16-byte misuse cannot smuggle through to a peer wire-decode-time reject.

SessionInitParams::cookie_signing_key field added. Caller supplies per-process secret; SessionInitParams::for_test seeds 32 × 0xAB. Cookie field semantics unchanged — still caller-owned wire bytes; the production Accepting side fills it via the new helper before install_session_actions.
2 unit tests for the HMAC helper: cookie_hmac_sha256_deterministic_16_byte_output + cookie_hmac_short_key_panics.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 46 → 48 (+2 HMAC unit tests); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
Cookie helper output deterministic (same key + peer_zid → same 16-byte cookie); divergent peer_zid yields divergent cookie. Short-key panic loud at construction site.

fixture_params + SessionInitParams::for_test both updated with cookie_signing_key to keep existing wire-byte assertions green; R57 send_init_ack_with_cookie test still passes because the bind closure does not regenerate the cookie inline — caller owns the field, and the test path keeps the literal cookie for byte-equiv reproducibility.
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 62 → 63.
carry_forward_bullets: R63a carry: transport extension chain emit on Init/Open/Join (§7 ext::QoS / ext::Auth / ext::MultiLink / ext::PatchType / ext::LowLatency). The SCE schema's <sce:tlv-chain> primitive supports the shape; wz init_body.scxml + open_body.scxml + join.scxml are not yet authored against it. Multi-session SCXML revision + verify-codegen.sh re-run + Layer 3 byte-equiv extension. Real zenoh peer interop requires this.

R63b carry: inbound parser pass. R62's cookie helper is for the Accepting side's outbound InitAck only; the Initiator-side InitAck decode path that pulls the peer-issued cookie off the wire and stages it into SessionInitParams.cookie for OpenSyn echo is not yet wired. Currently the Initiator's cookie field is caller-supplied at install time — production needs the inbound parser to populate it from the actual peer InitAck.
R63c carry: wz-runtime-lwip resolution still open from R60. R63 cluster bundles all three lwip-related carries (resolution decision + extension chain + inbound parser).
Hal time mock (R61b) and R40 strip workaround long-term decision both still open from prior rounds.

Round 64.1.decision_summary: CI Layer A hotfix — cargo install --bin mnemosyne-cli failed ambiguous-package; added --package mnemosyne-cli to resolve. First CI run after R63 push surfaced the gap.
changes_bullets: GitHub Actions CI run 25965126663 reported success (5m13s) but `mnemosyne-cli install failed; Layer A skipped` warning; the cargo install step ran `--bin mnemosyne-cli` against a multi-package workspace (audit-broadcast-bench / cascade-measurement / codegen-prototype / mnemosyne-cli / mnemosyne-mcp / schema-bench / ...) and got `error: multiple packages with binaries found`.

.github/workflows/ci.yml mnemosyne install step amended: `cargo install --git ... --branch main --package mnemosyne-cli --bin mnemosyne-cli`. The --package flag disambiguates the upstream workspace; --bin alone scopes the binary name but not the package selection.
Doc-comment in the workflow step expanded to record the failure mode + the R64.1 retrospect so a future contributor does not regress to the --bin-only form.
verification_bullets: Pre-R64.1 CI run (25965126663): conclusion=success, Layer A skipped via the warning + GITHUB_ENV MNEMOSYNE_SKIP=1 fallback. Layer B/C/D ran cleanly.

Round 64.2.decision_summary: CI Layer A re-hotfix — R64.1's --package flag was rejected by cargo install; correct shape is positional CRATE name + --bin disambiguator.
changes_bullets: CI run 25965296408 (R64.1 verification): mnemosyne-cli install still failed; stderr surfaced `error: unexpected argument '--package' found` from cargo install (R64.1 retrospect: I added --package without verifying it was a valid `cargo install` flag; cargo install's Usage line documents CRATE as positional, not behind --package).

.github/workflows/ci.yml mnemosyne install step amended to positional form: `cargo install --git ... --branch main --bin mnemosyne-cli mnemosyne-cli`. The trailing positional `mnemosyne-cli` selects the workspace crate; `--bin mnemosyne-cli` disambiguates the binary (the upstream crate may ship multiple binaries).

Doc-comment in the workflow step expanded to record both the R64.1 failure mode AND the R64.2 correct shape, so a future contributor cannot regress to either of the two broken variants (--bin-only ambiguous-package OR --package unexpected-argument).

changes_bullets: CI run 25965805229 (R64.3 verification): Install protoc + Install mnemosyne-cli both passed (R64.3 hotfix worked); Layer A executed validate-workspace and failed with `GENERATED.md=STALE`. docs/GENERATED.md is .gitignored so CI's fresh checkout sees no cascade output; local environment has it auto-regenerated by every atomic mutate.

.github/workflows/ci.yml: new `Regenerate docs/GENERATED.md (CI fresh-checkout cascade)` step inserted between mnemosyne-cli install and Layer A validate. Runs `mnemosyne-cli generate-docs` to restore the cascade invariant the local mutate path maintains automatically.

R64 series final retrospect (now 4 hotfixes deep): R64.1 wrong cargo install flag / R64.2 right flag but missing build dep / R64.3 protoc apt install / R64.4 GENERATED.md cascade. R64.1-R64.3 were local-detectable (cargo install --help + dry-run + local actionlint); R64.4 is fundamentally CI-vs-local environment-shape divergence (local cascade vs fresh-checkout absence) and required a CI run to surface.

Round 65.decision_summary: CI-equivalent local gate via scripts/run-ci.sh + pre-push delegation — process fix for R64.1's guess-and-push pattern. Hook now mirrors every CI lane.
changes_bullets: scripts/run-ci.sh (~165 lines) authored as the SSoT for CI lane invocation. Wraps Layer 0 actionlint (optional, skip-graceful) + Layer A mnemosyne validate + Layer B verify-codegen.sh per codec (with R44 LE-divergence allowlist) + Layer C1 cargo test --workspace + Layer C2 cargo clippy --deny warnings + Layer D validate-deploy.sh. Supports --skip-codegen + --layer <NAME> flags for partial runs during development.

.githooks/pre-push rewritten to delegate to scripts/run-ci.sh. The hook now blocks push when ANY required lane fails locally; CI yaml regressions like R64.1's `--package` rejection would be caught before reaching the GitHub Actions runner.

Round 70.decision_summary: Crypto safety hardening — SigningKey newtype + Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> drop-wipe + RFC 4231 HMAC-SHA256 test vectors. Closes the 3rd-review #4 crypto-secret-in-plain-memory defect.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml: zeroize = '1' with zeroize_derive feature added. RustCrypto-ecosystem standard for cryptographic secret lifecycle.

wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: new SigningKey struct wrapping Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>; SigningKey::new(bytes) validates >= 32 bytes at construct (RFC §5.M) and returns Err(SigningKeyTooShort) instead of panicking at HMAC site. Drop wipes the heap allocation — cookie key bytes no longer linger in core dumps / heap-leak windows / panic backtraces.

Manual Debug impl on SigningKey: shows length + literal '<redacted>' for bytes. Prevents log / panic stack-trace leakage; a regression to #[derive(Debug)] would surface in `signing_key_debug_redacts_bytes` test.
SessionInitParams::cookie_signing_key field type Vec<u8> → SigningKey. for_test fixture + integration test fixture_params both construct via SigningKey::new.

generate_cookie_hmac_sha256 split: pure compute_hmac_sha256_full(key, data) -> [u8; 32] primitive separate from the 16-byte wire truncation. The split lets RFC 4231 test vectors cross-check the underlying HMAC without entangling the cookie-truncation slice.
2 new RFC 4231 test cases (TC1 + TC2) cross-check against the canonical HMAC-SHA256 vectors. If RustCrypto's hmac/sha2 ever regresses on us the assertion fires loud.
Removed signing_key_short_panics test; replaced with signing_key_short_returns_err matching the new typed-error path.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 46 → 48 (R70 added 2 RFC vector tests + signing_key_short_returns_err + signing_key_debug_redacts_bytes; replaces 1 deleted panic test, net +3).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
RFC 4231 TC1 + TC2 assertions pass against compute_hmac_sha256_full: byte-equiv with the spec test vectors.
SigningKey Debug output verified to redact bytes (test asserts '<redacted>' present + hex absent).
validate_workspace local: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 72 → 73.
carry_forward_bullets: R70b carry: RFC 4231 TC3-TC7 coverage. R70 lands TC1+TC2 (smallest + 'Jefe' canonical pair); the remaining test cases (long key / long data / truncated MAC scenarios) extend the regression surface without changing the production code path.
R71 = wz-runtime-tokio-test-support separate crate split (encapsulation hardening; replaces _test_support feature flag).
R67 = R63a SCXML extension chain authoring (Phase Z prototype wire-spec gap).
R68 = R63b Initiator-side inbound parser.
R69 = Hal time-mock injection.

Round 70b.decision_summary: RFC 4231 TC3-TC7 coverage + §5.I intrinsic registry caveats — closes the 3rd-review crypto safety gaps (RFC test vector coverage + spec audit-trace).
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests module: 5 new test fns covering RFC 4231 TC3-TC7. TC3 uniform-byte key+data block-mix; TC4 sequential-byte key off-by-one; TC5 truncation invariant for the 16-byte cookie wire-shape (asserts both full 32-byte MAC + the [..16] truncated form); TC6 block-size+ key triggers hash-key-first path; TC7 both key and data exceed block size.

TC5 truncation test pins the wire-spec invariant that `generate_cookie_hmac_sha256` returns the first 16 bytes; a regression that returned a different slice would surface here AND in any downstream cookie wire interop.

check_lease_deadline reads SessionLinkActions::last_inbound_keepalive_at (R72b slot), compares now.duration_since(stamp) against params.lease via the lease_in_seconds unit selector, and engine.process_event(SessionFsmUnicastEvent::LeaseExpired) on `>= lease` so the session-fsm `lease.expired -> Closing(Expired)` transition fires

expired scenario also asserts trace side effects (close_reason=Expired, send_close_frame_with_reason=1, stop_keepalive_worker=1, stop_lease_monitor=1) matching session_fsm_coverage.rs::r61 lease-expired path so the helper inherits the existing FSM-edge verification
carry_forward_bullets: established_at slot — session-fsm §2.5 specifies lease counts from Established entry, but SessionLinkActions has no `established_at: Mutex<Option<Instant>>` slot yet; NoBaseline branch defers the decision while the slot stays None. A follow-up round adds the slot + Established-state-entry hook so pre-KeepAlive sessions can still expire on session-start fallback timing

production driver loop with timeout — check_lease_deadline is one unit; the loop pairing poll_and_dispatch_one (which blocks on peer events) with periodic lease checks needs tokio::select! against a sleep deadline so a silent peer does not pin the poller. R76b drive_session_until_terminal scope.
R71b/R77b singleton race — the mega-test pattern is still load-bearing (process-global INSTALLED OnceLock + Lua engine); option 4 direct-codec rebind OR SCE bind_native_object upstream still pending.

Frame inbound also resets the lease? — R72b populates `last_inbound_keepalive_at` only on InboundFrame::KeepAlive. Whether established-session Frame traffic should also count as liveness is a wire-spec discussion (zenoh-pico transport.h:213 wording is `KEEP_ALIVE` specifically); R77 honours R72b's KeepAlive-only design without bundling that question.

production driver loop with timeout — check_lease_deadline is one unit; the loop pairing poll_and_dispatch_one (which blocks on peer events) with periodic lease checks needs tokio::select! against a sleep deadline so a silent peer does not pin the poller. R76b drive_session_until_terminal scope.

Round 78.decision_summary: Accepting-side path integration test — listener handshake via poll_and_dispatch_one terminates at Established.

hand-craft two inbound wires: craft_initsyn_wire (parent_flags=FLAG_T_INIT_S, no FLAG_T_INIT_A, 10-byte frame with version/cbyte/zid/sn_res/batch_size and no cookie) + craft_opensyn_wire (parent_flags=0x00, 4-byte frame with lease=0/initial_sn=0/cookie_len=0)

QueueDriver + NoopOutboundDriver test scaffolding mirrors session_fsm_driver_loop.rs from R76 — the helpers are duplicated rather than extracted because mid-flight scaffold extraction would obscure the per-test isolation rules

Established.onentry side effects asserted (enable_rx_tx_regions=1, start_lease_monitor=1, start_keepalive_worker=1) matching session_fsm_coverage.rs::r61 listener-path expectations — R78 inherits R61's FSM-edge verification but reaches it through the R76 driver helper rather than direct engine.process_event(E::InitSynReceived)

complements the R76 Initiator-side Rx(InitAck) scenario — confirms poll_and_dispatch_one handles both handshake halves symmetrically (helper does not discriminate Initiator vs Accepting; the FSM does)

carry_forward_bullets: Accepting-side InitSyn cookie generation — send_init_ack_with_cookie currently reads params.cookie which the fixture leaves empty. QueueDriver / NoopOutboundDriver duplication across session_fsm_driver_loop.rs + session_fsm_accepting_path.rs — extraction into wz-runtime-tokio-test-support would consolidate ~80 lines but risks the same singleton-race contagion the mega-test pattern guards against. InboundStart dispatch in production — the test calls engine.process_event(E::InboundStart) directly because the production caller (socket-accept handler) has not been wired yet. Phase W lwIP or AP-side listener bind needs an explicit InboundStart hook on accept().

cookie wire field in OpenSyn is empty (cookie_len=0) — RFC §5.M expects the Initiator to echo the cookie it received in InitAck; R78 omits the echo because the test stages only OpenSyn arrival without a prior InitAck-out + cookie-in round-trip. End-to-end cookie echo verification belongs in wz-integration-tests Layer 3 (already covered by layer3_open_body wire-interop tests).

Round 81.decision_summary: R76b drive_session_until_terminal — production session loop composes poll_and_dispatch_one + lease deadline via tokio::select!.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: added DriverOutcome enum (Terminated / IterationLimit) and async drive_session_until_terminal helper after check_lease_deadline; loop body composes poll_and_dispatch_one with a tokio::select! race against tokio::time::sleep(remaining lease window)
termination at iteration top: engine.is_in_final_state() -> Terminated; max_iters reached -> IterationLimit; production callers pass max_iters=None for unlimited, test callers cap it

lease branch: reads last_inbound_keepalive_at, computes deadline = stamp + lease (lease_in_seconds-aware), sleeps for saturating_duration_since(now) of the remaining window, then invokes check_lease_deadline(actions, engine, Instant::now()) on the sleep-wins branch; if stamp slot is None (no inbound keepalive yet) falls through to a plain poll_and_dispatch_one await (no lease watchdog)

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_drive_session.rs: new test file with 4 #[tokio::test] fns covering loop wiring shape — already-terminal engine surfaces Terminated, max_iters=0 surfaces IterationLimit, staged LinkEvent::Lost drives Terminated via poll branch, short-lease + HangingDriver advances FSM past Established via sleep branch

HangingDriver scaffold (std::future::pending::<LinkEvent>().await) models a silent peer so the lease branch is the only forward path; QueueDriver mirrors R76 driver_loop test scaffolding (intentional duplication, scaffolding-extraction is a separate retrospective)
closes R76 honest weakness #1 carry ("poll_and_dispatch_one is a production unit, not a production loop") — R76b is the loop wrapper that R76 was missing

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_drive_session: 4 passed in 0.03s (wall-clock lease test stable at 20ms lease + 8 max_iters)
cargo test --workspace: 97 passed / 0 failed (93 -> 97, +4 = exact R76b new test count)
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean
scripts/run-ci.sh: Layer A (validate-workspace) + Layer B (verify-codegen) + Layer C1 (test) + Layer C2 (clippy) + Layer D (deploy schema) all pass
lease branch fires under HangingDriver: scenario 4 asserts FSM advances past Established within wall-clock budget (lease=20ms, 8 max_iters allows ~160ms total)
carry_forward_bullets: Time-paused deterministic test for the lease branch — the current scenario 4 relies on wall-clock 20ms; tokio::time::pause + advance would make it deterministic, but the production code mixes tokio::time::sleep (paused-aware) with check_lease_deadline's Instant::now (std clock, NOT paused). Unifying the time source requires either parametrising drive_session_until_terminal with a clock trait or porting check_lease_deadline to tokio::time::Instant. Defer to a separate round.

Cancel-safety boundary docstring added — in-tree drivers (TcpDriver/UdpDriver/QueueDriver/HangingDriver) are cancel-safe at the poll_event boundary, but extern target-plugin drivers (Phase W) may not be. Phase W integration must audit cancel-safety per driver.

max_iters=None production case untested directly — tests cap iterations for determinism; the unlimited code path is exercised only through cargo's overall workspace runtime. A future end-to-end integration (R76c?) with real TCP loopback could verify the unlimited shape under a finite total runtime budget.

drive_session_until_terminal does not surface the inner DriverLoopOutcome (AdvancedFsm / SideEffectOnly / ParseError / LinkLost) per iteration — production telemetry / logging callers would want that. Add a per-iteration observer callback in a follow-up round if needed.

Round 82.decision_summary: R74 Frame.payload application-layer dispatch — parse_frame_payload + NetworkMessage{Request, Unknown}; DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload replaces the silent payload-drop in poll_and_dispatch_one's Frame arm.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: NetworkMessage enum (Request(Box<wz_codecs::request::Request>) / Unknown { mid, body }) + parse_frame_payload(bytes) -> Result<Vec<NetworkMessage>, CodecError> helper that loops over the Frame.payload batch, dispatching N_MID_REQUEST (0x1C) to wz_codecs::request::Request::decode and absorbing other MIDs as Unknown { mid, body } that terminates the batch loop (no length-aware decoder for unknown bodies means cursor cannot safely advance past them).

DriverLoopOutcome gains FramePayload { reliable, sn, messages, has_ext, extensions } variant; SideEffectOnly is now KeepAlive-only. The prior #[derive(Debug)] is replaced by manual Debug impls on both DriverLoopOutcome and NetworkMessage because wz-codecs structs (Request / ExtEntry) only derive Default per sce-codegen output — the manual impl prints variant kind + ext_count + body_len without recursing into codec fields so existing {outcome:?} test assertions keep working.

poll_and_dispatch_one Frame arm rewired: previously bucketed under None -> DriverLoopOutcome::SideEffectOnly (payload silently dropped). Now matches InboundFrame::Frame fields, runs parse_frame_payload, surfaces FramePayload on Ok or ParseError(InboundParseError::Codec) + Engine::process_event(E::FramingError) on Err. Application-layer parse failure is conservatively treated as a transport-FSM framing.error so the session moves to Closing on malformed batch payload (revisitable per carry).

inbound_to_fsm_event docstring extended: Frame None branch now documents 'application-layer dispatch via parse_frame_payload' instead of 'drop'. KeepAlive None branch unchanged. The function itself still returns None for both — the dispatch fan-out lives in poll_and_dispatch_one.

wire_const::N_MID_REQUEST (0x1C) constant added. The other 6 network MIDs (PUSH 0x1D, RESPONSE 0x1B, DECLARE 0x1E, OAM 0x1F, RESPONSE_FINAL 0x1A, INTEREST 0x19 per zenoh-pico network.h:33-39) are documented in NetworkMessage rustdoc but not declared as constants until their decoders land — YAGNI per the existing wire_const philosophy where every named constant has at least one match-arm consumer.

Tests added — 4 parse_frame_payload unit tests in session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: empty payload returns empty Vec; Unknown MID (0x1D) absorbs remainder; Request envelope MID 0x1C dispatches to Request::decode (first record is Request); truncated Request body returns CodecError. 3 r74_* poll_and_dispatch_one integration scenarios in session_fsm_driver_loop.rs: empty-payload Frame surfaces FramePayload{messages=[]}, Unknown-MID payload surfaces FramePayload{messages=[Unknown{mid:0x1D, body}]}, malformed Request payload surfaces ParseError + FSM transitions SentInitSyn -> Closing.

Closes R73 + R76 carry 'Application-layer Frame payload dispatch (R74-tier) — DriverLoopOutcome::SideEffectOnly variant on Frame currently drops the payload; downstream consumer needs the msg_put/del/query/response codec dispatch'. The dispatch wiring is now in place; envelope codec coverage remains REQUEST-only until SCE Push/Response/Declare/OAM/Interest SCXMLs land.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio green.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_inbound_dispatch: 11 passed (7 prior + 4 R74 unit). cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_driver_loop: 8 passed (5 R76 + 3 R74).
cargo test --workspace: 104 passed / 0 failed (97 -> 104, +7 = exact R74 new test count: 4 unit + 3 integration).

cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean. Two clippy regressions encountered + fixed inside the round: large-enum-variant on NetworkMessage::Request resolved via Box<Request>; field-reassign-with-default in test code resolved via struct update syntax (`Request { header: 0x1C, ..Request::default() }`).
scripts/run-ci.sh: Layer A (validate-workspace) + Layer B (verify-codegen) + Layer C1 (test) + Layer C2 (clippy) + Layer D (deploy schema) all pass — `run-ci: all required layers pass`.

wz_codecs::request encode/decode byte-count discrepancy discovered empirically: Request::default() with header=0x1C encodes N bytes that Request::decode consumes M < N. The R74 batch loop absorbs the residual as Unknown rather than misinterpreting it. The unit test parse_frame_payload_dispatches_request_mid_to_request_decoder asserts 'first record is Request' (parsed[0]) instead of strict parsed.len()==1; the discrepancy is documented in carry forward as a wz-codecs/sce-codegen issue out of R74 scope.

validate_workspace baseline preserved: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync. T3 warn 856 / T4 info 427 unchanged (no doc mutation). Atomic ledger entries 94 -> 95 (this Round entry); sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: wz_codecs::request encode/decode roundtrip discrepancy — Request::default() with header=0x1C produces bytes where Request::decode consumes a different (smaller) byte count, leaving residual bytes that the R74 batch loop absorbs as Unknown. The contract should be 'encode then decode consumes exactly the encoded bytes' regardless of state. Likely root cause: Wireexpr default-state encode/decode mismatch OR RequestVariant Default-arm tag round-trip. A focused round on the wz-codecs codegen or Wireexpr default state would close this; without it, real production Request payloads (where header is set and inner body is meaningful) may roundtrip cleanly, but the codec contract is currently soft on default state.

Network-envelope codec coverage — wz_codecs::request is the only authored envelope. The other 6 network MIDs (PUSH 0x1D, RESPONSE 0x1B, DECLARE 0x1E, OAM 0x1F, RESPONSE_FINAL 0x1A, INTEREST 0x19) decode as Unknown until their SCE codec SCXMLs land. PUSH is the highest-priority next target (pub/sub data path = put/del without request-reply correlation); RESPONSE follows for request-reply parity. Each new envelope codec round bumps a wire_const::N_MID_* constant and adds a parse_frame_payload match arm.

Application-layer parse error -> transport FramingError mapping conservative — R74 treats any CodecError inside parse_frame_payload as a transport FramingError that closes the session. Stricter wire-spec reading might preserve the session and only log application-layer batch failures (the Frame transport envelope itself parsed OK). Revisit if production deploys need batch resilience or if integration testing surfaces sessions closing on transient app-layer noise.

FramePayload outcome not yet consumed by drive_session_until_terminal — R81's loop discards the per-iteration DriverLoopOutcome (matches all variants with `=> {}`). A future round (composing on R81 carry #4 'per-iteration observer callback') wires FramePayload.messages to a consumer registry — pub/sub topic dispatcher / Lua bridge / Rust callback. Without that wiring, R74 surfaces the payload at the API boundary but no one reads it yet.
